nodes.label: Why? (Poem)
nodes.description: A poem about a child asking questions about the world

nodes.label: Theme: Curiosity
nodes.description: The poem celebrates the habit of asking questions

nodes.label: Questions in the Poem
nodes.description: Why is the sky blue? Why does the wind blow? Why does it rain?

nodes.label: Message
nodes.description: Never stop asking questions -- it is how we learn and grow

nodes.label: Alice in Wonderland (Story)
nodes.description: A story about a curious girl who enters a magical land

nodes.label: Alice
nodes.description: A curious young girl who follows a white rabbit

nodes.label: The White Rabbit
nodes.description: A rabbit in a coat with a pocket watch, always in a hurry

nodes.label: Key Events
nodes.description: Alice falls down the rabbit hole, grows big and shrinks small

nodes.label: Grammar: Question Words
nodes.description: Words used to ask questions: Who, What, Where, When, Why, Which, How

nodes.label: Wh-Words
nodes.description: What (things), Where (place), When (time), Who (person), Why (reason), Which (choice)

nodes.label: How
nodes.description: Asks about manner, amount, or degree: How far? How many? How old?

nodes.label: Question Mark (?)
nodes.description: Every question sentence must end with a question mark

nodes.label: Connecting Theme
nodes.description: The poem, story, and grammar all connect through the idea of asking questions and being curious
